input.initialPrompt:
I have an HTTP-based C# MCP server built with ASP.NET Core.
How do I write integration tests that test the full MCP protocol
over HTTP without spinning up a real server?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to write integration tests for a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server built with ASP.NET Core, testing the full MCP protocol over HTTP without spinning up a real server. This is a knowledge/guidance question, so I can answer directly without needing to explore files.

The standard approach in ASP.NET Core is to use `WebApplicationFactory<T>` from `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing`, which creates an in-memory test server. You can then use the `HttpClient` from that factory to send MCP protocol messages (JSON-RPC over HTTP with SSE or Streamable HTTP transport).


I'll walk through a practical example showing how to set up the factory, configure it for MCP, and then demonstrate sending test requests with assertions on the responses.
